title: Finding a north component from a single bearing
problem: A ship sails on a bearing of $035°$ for 100 km. How far north of its starting point is the ship? Give your answer correct to 1 decimal place.

steps.instruction: Draw a diagram and identify the right-angled triangle.
steps.working: Draw a north-south reference line at the starting point. The ship's path makes a $35°$ angle with north (measured clockwise). Drop a perpendicular from the end of the path to the north-south line. This forms a right-angled triangle with the $35°$ angle at the starting point, the 100 km path as the hypotenuse, and the north component as the adjacent side.

steps.instruction: Write the trigonometric ratio for the north component.
steps.working:
The north component is adjacent to the $35°$ angle and the hypotenuse is 100 km, so:
$$\cos(35°) = \frac{\text{North}}{100}$$
$$\text{North} = 100\cos(35°)$$

answer: The ship is $81.9$ km north of its starting point.

title: Finding straight-line distance after a two-leg journey
problem: A ship sails 80 km on a bearing of $060°$ and then 60 km on a bearing of $150°$. Find the straight-line distance from the starting point.

steps.instruction: Find the north and east components of Leg 1.
Find the north and east components of Leg 2.
Find the total north and east displacements.
Apply Pythagoras' theorem to find the straight-line distance.

answer: The ship is $100$ km from its starting point.

title: Finding distance and back-bearing for a two-leg journey
problem: A boat sails 30 km due west and then 40 km due south. Find the straight-line distance from the starting point and the true bearing of the starting point from the boat's final position.

steps.working: The boat travels 30 km west (east component $= -30$ km) and 40 km south (north component $= -40$ km). From the starting point, the boat's final position is 30 km west and 40 km south.

steps.instruction: Find the straight-line distance using Pythagoras' theorem.
Find the direction from the boat back to the start.
steps.working: From the boat, the start is 30 km east and 40 km north. This direction is north-east, so the bearing is between $000°$ and $090°$.

steps.instruction: Calculate the angle from north towards east.
steps.working:
$$\tan(\theta) = \frac{\text{east}}{\text{north}} = \frac{30}{40} = 0.75$$
$$\theta = \arctan(0.75) \approx 36.9°$$
The bearing of the start from the boat is approximately $037°$.

answer: The straight-line distance is $50$ km; the starting point is on a bearing of $037°$ from the boat.
